claim: For all integers n, n^2 is congruent to 0 or 1 modulo 4.
For all integers n, n^2-n is even.
For all integers n, n^2+n is even.
For all integers n, n^2 is congruent to 0, 1, or 4 modulo 8.
For all odd integers n, n^2 is congruent to 1 modulo 8.
For all odd integers n, n^2-1 is divisible by 8.
For all odd integers n, n^2-1 is divisible by 16.
For all odd integers n, n^2-1 is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, n^5-n is divisible by 30.
For all integers n, n^7-n is divisible by 42.
For all integers n, n^11-n is divisible by 66.
For all integers n, n^4 is congruent to 0 or 1 modulo 16.
For all odd integers n, n^4 is congruent to 1 modulo 16.
For all odd integers n, n^4-1 is divisible by 16.
For all integers n, gcd(n,n^2-1)=1.
For all integers n, gcd(n-1,n+1) divides 2.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2-1,n^2+1) divides 2.
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by 2.
For all prime integers n, n^2-1 is divisible by 24.
For all prime integers n, n^2 is congruent to 1 modulo 24.
For all prime integers n, n^4 is congruent to 1 modulo 240.
For all integers n, n(n-1)/2 is an integer.
For all integers n, n(n-1)(n-2) is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, n(n-1)(n-2)(n-3) is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, n(n-1)(n-2)(n-3)(n-4) is divisible by 120.
For all integers n, n^2+n+1 is odd.
For all integers n, n^2+n+1 is coprime to n.
For all integers n, n^2+n+1 is coprime to n+1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2+n+1,n^2-n+1) divides 3.
For all integers n, n^2+1 is never divisible by 4.
For all odd integers n, n^2+1 is congruent to 2 modulo 8.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^2-1,3) is not equal to 1 only rarely.
For all integers n, n^2(n^2-1) is divisible by 12.
For all integers n, n^2(n^2-1) is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, n^4-n^2 is divisible by 24.
For all nonzero integers n, phi(n) is even except when |n|=1 or 2.
For all integers n, phi(n^2) is divisible by phi(n) when n is nonzero.
For all integers n, phi(n^2) is at least phi(n) when n is nonzero.
For all positive integers n, tau(n^2) is odd.
For all positive integers n, tau(n^2) is at least tau(n).
For all positive integers n, sigma(n) is odd if and only if n is a square or twice a square.
For all integers n, n^3-n has at least one factor among n-1,n,n+1 divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n^2+2 cannot be congruent to 0 modulo 4.
For all integers n, n^2 modulo 16 belongs to {0,1,4,9}.
For all odd integers n, n^2 modulo 16 belongs to {1,9}.
For all prime integers n, n^2+n+1 is not divisible by n.
For all prime integers n, gcd(n,n^2-1)=1.
For all integers n, (n^2-1)^2 is congruent to 0 modulo 16.
For all integers n, (n^2-1)^2 is divisible by 64.
For all integers n, n^3-n equals the product of three consecutive integers.
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by the gcd of n-1 and n+1.
For all integers n, if 3 divides n then gcd(n^2+n+1,n^2-n+1)=3.
For all positive integers n, phi(n) is less than or equal to n.
